channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Hungary’s Long Road Back
item.description: When Péter Magyar took the stage in Budapest on the night of 12 April, he told the crowd they had ‘liberated Hungary’. The hyperbole seemed justified. His party, Tisza, had won a parliamentary supermajority on the highest turnout since Hungary’s first free election in 1990, ending 16 years of increasingly autocratic rule. An autocracy built […]

item.title: Before the Flood, Jannat Carried Books. After the Flood, She Carried Dirty Dishes
item.description: When catastrophic floods swept through the Haor wetlands of Sunamganj in 2022, they destroyed far more than homes and crops. They shattered childhoods. Jannat was only nine years old when floodwater swallowed her family’s house, farmland, and livestock. Like thousands of displaced families in northeastern Bangladesh, they took shelter in a school building converted into […]

item.title: Gaza’s Deepening Health Crisis Leaves Hospitals Overwhelmed
item.description: Despite the implementation of a ceasefire between Hamas and Israel last October, Israeli forces continue to launch airstrikes into the Occupied Palestinian Territory. This has resulted in extensive destruction of infrastructure, loss of human life and exacerbating immense health needs amid an increasingly strained health system in Gaza. Recent months have marked a significant escalation […]

item.title: ‘Every Bird Counts:’ How city residents can help protect migratory birds
item.description: Did you know that millions of birds may be migrating silently overhead right now as you read this – and you don’t even need to leave the house to see them?

item.title: Ukraine: Over 3,000 attacks on healthcare since full-scale Russian invasion
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has verified more than 3,000 attacks on healthcare in Ukraine since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in February 2022, the UN agency reported on Friday.

item.title: ‘Victims need a voice, assistance and justice’: UN victims’ rights advocate calls for stronger action
item.description: The United Nations Victims’ Rights Advocate has called for stronger and sustained action on behalf of the people she serves across the UN system.

item.title: Lebanon war leaves families foraging for food despite ceasefire
item.description: The fragile ceasefire in Lebanon hasn’t prevented “ongoing killing and displacement”, with villages in the south of the country rendered completely unrecognizable after Israeli strikes, aid teams reported on Friday.

item.title: Wider hantavirus outbreak risk is ‘absolutely low’, insists UN health agency
item.description: The risk of hantavirus spreading to the general population is “absolutely low”, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) stressed on Friday, as a flight attendant tested negative for the disease after coming into contact with an infected passenger from the cruise ship at the centre of the outbreak, who later died.

item.title: Malaria vaccine is saving children’s lives: WHO
item.description: New evidence shows that malaria vaccination is significantly reducing child deaths in Africa and could have an even greater impact as programmes expand, the World Health Organization (WHO) said on Friday.

item.title: Somalia teeters on the brink of catastrophe as hunger crisis deepens
item.description: “This is not a distant warning. This is a crisis that is unfolding right now and it's deepening quickly.”

item.title: World News in Brief: Haiti crisis continues, Gaza-West Bank update, Sir Richard Attenborough at 100
item.description: Haiti continues to face escalating violence and deepening insecurity, with gangs expanding their reach beyond Port-au-Prince and civilians increasingly caught between armed groups and security operations.

item.title: Central African Republic: Funding cuts put humanitarian support at risk
item.description: The Central African Republic (CAR) is making progress towards stability and security but major aid budget cuts threaten humanitarian operations there, a senior official with the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Friday in New York.

item.title: Nuclear ‘Close-Calls’ Prove Deterrence No Guarantee for Peace
item.description: The consequences of nuclear warfare would transcend borders and the impact would be felt across generations. Yet knowing this, member states, including nuclear-armed states, are increasingly flouting the nuclear taboo, while also relying heavily on deterrence to prevent fallout. Throughout the Cold War period, there were stories of nuclear “close calls”—moments where the world could […]

item.title: Empowering Youth Is the Fastest Path to Transforming Least Developed Countries
item.description: Rabab Fatima is United Nations Under Secretary General and High Representative for LDCs, LLDCs and SIDS

item.title: The Mideast Conflict Spreads—Beyond the Strait of Hormuz & towards the UN Cafeteria
item.description: The 10-month-old Middle East conflict—which has triggered a rise in the cost of living worldwide, and an increase in the prices of food, groceries and gasoline—is likely to impose burdens on hundreds of UN staffers, delegates, journalists and civil society representatives– and thousands more, during the General Assembly sessions beginning September. The proposed increases are […]
